
<details id="{model_hyphen}-{engine_hyphen}">

<summary>With the `"{engine_name}"` engine</summary>

We'll model satisfaction of householders under varying conditions.

```{r}
library(tidymodels)
library(ordered)
tidymodels_prefer()

house_data <-
  MASS::housing[rep(seq(nrow(MASS::housing)), MASS::housing$Freq), -5]
house_sample <- sort(sample(nrow(house_data), 12L))
house_train <- house_data[-house_sample, ]
house_test <- house_data[house_sample, ]
```

We can define the model with specific parameters:

```{r}
{model_abbr}_spec <-
  {model_name}({engine_args}) |>
  set_engine("{engine_name}") |>
  set_mode("classification")
{model_abbr}_spec
```

Now we create the model fit object:

```{r}
set.seed(1)
{model_abbr}_fit <- {model_abbr}_spec |> fit(Sat ~ Infl + Type + Cont, data = house_train)
{model_abbr}_fit
```

The holdout data can be predicted for {types_clause}:

```{r}
{types_chunk}
```

</details>
